items.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
items.summary: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

items.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
items.summary: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

items.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
items.summary: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

items.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
items.summary: Mistral AI, which offers some open source AI models, has raised significant funding since its creation in 2023, with the ambition to “put frontier AI in the hands of everyone.”

items.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
items.summary: Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. items.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
items.summary: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

items.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
items.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

items.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
items.summary: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
items.summary: At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. items.title: AI’s Volatile Power Use Quietly Tests Grid Limits
items.summary: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure is typically framed as an energy problem. items.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
items.summary: Midjourney has shown more of its futuristic medical scanner. It still hasn't shown much proof it works. items.summary: As the parent of two little girls, I often think about how their childhood is different from mine. The seven-year-old is learning about AI at school. items.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
items.summary: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

items.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
items.summary: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

items.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
items.summary: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

items.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
items.summary: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

items.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
items.summary: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. items.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

items.title: Trump gets OpenAI to offer US 5% stake, far lower than Sanders’ target
items.summary: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.

items.title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
items.summary: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
